steps.title: Understand the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence has a "While…" contrast: recent scholarship has undermined claims that his works were [blank] other Muslim philosophers. The second half confirms he was located thousands of miles from the center of Islamic thought. The blank describes a claim that scholarship has now undermined — so it must be something scholars previously believed but have now disproven, meaning his works actually did matter to contemporaries despite the geographic distance.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Work through all four choices:
  • Choice A — 'controversial among': "Controversial among" means causing dispute. The passage is about access and influence, not whether his works sparked disagreement. Scholarship undermining this claim would mean his works were not controversial — but the text never raises controversy as the issue. ✗
  • Choice B — 'antagonistic toward': "Antagonistic toward" means hostile toward. The blank describes Ibn Rushd's works' relationship to other philosophers, not hostility directed at them. The passage does not suggest his works attacked contemporaries. ✗
  • Choice C — 'imitated by': "Imitated by" would mean other philosophers copied his works. The passage says scholarship undermined this claim, implying scholars found his works were not widely imitated — but the text doesn't address imitation at all. ✗
  • Choice D — 'inconsequential to': "Inconsequential to" means not significant to. His remote location would logically lead to the claim that his works had no impact on Muslim contemporaries. Recent scholarship undermined that claim by showing his works did matter — exactly the tension the sentence sets up. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
